title: Redis key prefix inventory: 12 unique prefixes across services
evidence: coordinator:leader: (coordinator/coordinator.ts:390), health:{svc} (shared/core/redis/client.ts:1039), health:system:snapshot (shared/core/monitoring/enhanced-health-monitor.ts:544), health_state:{svc} (shared/core/resilience/expert-self-healing-manager.ts:405), lock:execution: (execution-engine/engine.ts:448), bridge:recovery: (execution-engine/types.ts:1170), bridge:recovery:corrupt: (execution-engine/strategies/cross-chain.strategy.ts:487), price:{chainId}:{addr} (unified-detector/chain-instance.ts:2065), dlq:{id} (shared/core/resilience/dead-letter-queue.ts:511), commit-reveal:{chain}:{hash} (execution-engine/services/commit-reveal.service.ts), ratelimit: (coordinator middleware), stream:* (shared/core/redis/streams.ts)
actual: 12 unique prefixes, no active collisions. Previous SA-1O-002 health:snapshot collision was fixed.
recommendation: Consider centralizing prefix inventory in a REDIS_KEY_REGISTRY.md for onboarding and auditing

title: health_state: prefix diverges from health: colon convention
evidence: expert-self-healing-manager.ts:405 uses `health_state:${serviceName}` with underscore. redis/client.ts:1039 uses `health:${serviceName}` with colon. enhanced-health-monitor.ts:544 uses `health:system:snapshot` with colon.
actual: health_state: uses underscore separator, breaking the established health: namespace convention
recommendation: Rename health_state: to health:state: for namespace consistency. Low risk — both use short TTLs (5min/1hr) and different read patterns.

title: service-ports.json $comment references stale port 8080 for execution-engine
evidence: shared/constants/service-ports.json:3 $comment says 'execution-engine uses port 8080 in Fly.io production'. Fly.io TOML (infrastructure/fly/execution-engine.toml:37) was already fixed by SA-1Q-001 to HEALTH_CHECK_PORT=3005, internal_port=3005, checks port=3005.
actual: Comment still says port 8080 — stale after SA-1Q-001 fix
recommendation: Update $comment to remove stale 8080 reference or simply note all ports match service-ports.json in production

title: ADR-022: No spread operators in hot-path tight loops
evidence: Grepped for spread operators in price-matrix.ts (moved to shared/core/src/caching/), provider-latency-tracker.ts, execution-engine/src/, unified-detector/src/. All spreads are in cold-path contexts: config merging, status getters ({...this.metrics}), one-shot array creation. keys.push(...validKeys) at cross-chain.strategy.ts:2005 is in a SCAN loop but bounded (MAX_RECOVERY_KEYS, SCAN COUNT 100) and is background recovery, not hot-path.
expected: No spread operators in loops on <50ms hot path
actual: Compliant. All spreads are in cold-path or bounded contexts.

title: ADR-022: RingBuffer uses Float64Array with O(1) record()
evidence: provider-latency-tracker.ts:71 RingBuffer class, line 80: this.buffer = new Float64Array(capacity), lines 83-94: record() uses direct index write + modular increment, all O(1).

title: ADR-033: Stale price thresholds correctly layered (5s L1, 30s detection)
evidence: ADR-033-stale-price-window.md:139 documents maxPriceAgeMs=30000 in DetectorConfig. PriceMatrix.getPriceWithFreshnessCheck default=5000ms (price-matrix.ts:717) is intentional per ADR-033 section on per-layer thresholds.

title: ADR-002: No direct HTTP inter-service calls — Redis Streams only
evidence: Grep for http://localhost:300[0-6] in services/**/*.ts. Only match: coordinator CORS allowlist (middleware/index.ts:86) — browser origin, not inter-service call.
expected: All inter-service comms via Redis Streams

title: ADR-018: Circuit breaker failureThreshold=5 default confirmed everywhere
evidence: Defaults at circuit-breaker.ts:408 (5), self-healing-manager.ts:36 (5), execution-engine/types.ts:683 (5), index.ts:99 (5), notifier.ts:47 (5), uniswapx-filler.ts:188 (5), backrun-bundle-builder.ts:183 (5). mev-share-event-listener.ts:233 intentionally uses maxReconnectAttempts (documented).

title: ADR-005: SharedArrayBuffer confirmed in PriceMatrix with fallback
evidence: price-matrix.ts:224 private sharedBuffer: SharedArrayBuffer|null. Line 438-439 creates SharedArrayBuffer. Line 454 falls back to ArrayBuffer. Seqlock protocol for thread safety (line 898).

title: Fly.io cross-chain-detector.toml missing /ready health check
evidence: infrastructure/fly/cross-chain-detector.toml has only [checks.health] (path=/health, port=3006). Other non-coordinator services have both [checks.health] and [checks.ready]. The cross-chain service implements readyCheck (index.ts:69-72) requiring isRunning + redisConnected + chainsMonitored > 0, but Fly.io never probes it.
expected: Both /health and /ready checks in Fly.io TOML
actual: Only /health configured. /ready endpoint exists in code but is not probed by Fly.io.
recommendation: Add [checks.ready] to cross-chain-detector.toml: grace_period=60s, interval=15s, path=/ready, port=3006, timeout=5s, type=http

title: All 8 Dockerfiles use node:22-alpine — version alignment confirmed
evidence: All Dockerfiles under services/ use FROM node:22-alpine. Multi-stage builds use it for both builder and production stages. Matches engines >=22.0.0.

title: Fly.io TOML ports and health paths align with service-ports.json
evidence: coordinator=3000(/api/health), coordinator-standby=3000(/api/health), asia-fast=3001(/health,/ready), l2-turbo=3002(/health,/ready), high-value=3003(/health,/ready), solana=3004(/health,/ready), cross-chain=3006(/health), execution-engine=3005(/health,/ready). All match service-ports.json.

title: Docker Compose health checks and ports consistent across 3 compose files
evidence: docker-compose.yml, docker-compose.partition.yml, docker-compose.testnet.yml all use correct ports. Partition compose uses internal 3001 per container (P2-25 documented). All health intervals standardized to 15s per ADR-007. Coordinator uses /api/health, others use /health.

title: Execution engine: shutdown(45s) > drain(30s) > per-disconnect(8s) > redis-connect(3s)
evidence: index.ts:434 drain=30000, line 435 POST_DRAIN_CLEANUP_BUFFER=15000, line 446 shutdownTimeoutMs=45000. types.ts:868 SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=8000. Redis connectTimeout=3000. Runtime validation at line 438.

title: Partition services: SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS(8s) > closeServer(1s), > redis-connect(3s)
evidence: health-server.ts:501 SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=8000. runner.ts:255 closeServerWithTimeout(1000). Redis connectTimeout=3000.

title: Cross-chain: 2x per-disconnect(4s)=8s < service-shutdown(10s) > redis-connect(3s)
evidence: detector.ts:515 SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=4000 per disconnect. Two sequential disconnects (lines 525,529): 8s total < 10s service shutdown default.

title: Monolith: perWorkerTimeout(28s) < shutdown(30s) — properly fixed
evidence: index.ts:66 SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=30000. worker-manager.ts:358-360 perWorkerTimeout=shutdownTimeoutMs-2000=28000. SA-4R5 FIX comment at line 355 documents the fix.
